landmarks.name: World's Tallest Thermometer
landmarks.spoken: Billboard: The world's tallest thermometer ahead in Baker. Over a hundred feet of neon telling you exactly how hot the Mojave is. The answer is always too hot.

landmarks.name: Alien Fresh Jerky
landmarks.spoken: Billboard: Alien Fresh Jerky, next exit in Baker. They will not say who the jerky is made from. The little green fellow on the sign is not talking.

landmarks.name: The Mad Greek
landmarks.spoken: Billboard: The Mad Greek ahead in Baker. Gyros and baklava in the middle of the desert. Trust the Mojave -- it has surprised you before.

landmarks.name: Zzyzx Road
landmarks.spoken: Billboard: Coming up, the exit for Zzyzx Road -- the last word in the dictionary, out here in the sand. There is a strange story behind that name, and a lake you would never expect.

landmarks.name: Daggett
landmarks.source: OpenStreetMap place=village node 150949205, projected onto the leg's OpenRouteService driving-hgv route

landmarks.name: Peggy Sue's Fifties Diner
landmarks.spoken: Billboard: Peggy Sue's fifties diner ahead near Yermo. Chrome, a jukebox, a diner cat, and a pond full of concrete dinosaurs out back. Rock and roll never checked out.

landmarks.name: Lenwood

landmarks.name: Calico Ghost Town
landmarks.spoken: Billboard: Calico Ghost Town ahead near Barstow -- a real silver boomtown, half restored and half falling down, and, they will tell you, thoroughly haunted.
